audience: Training providers, consultants, HR and L&D teams, and SMEs with an existing course, workshop or internal training programme
primaryDutchSearchIntent: Organisaties uitleggen hoe ze een bestaande liveopleiding verantwoord modulariseren tot herbruikbare, toegankelijke en onderhoudbare video-content

searchIntent.question: How can an organisation turn an existing live training course into reusable, accessible and maintainable video content?
searchIntent.geographicContext: English-speaking organisations in Belgium and neighbouring markets

directAnswer: Turn an existing training course into video content by redistributing its learning objectives, explanations, demonstrations and exercises across small modules that work independently. Do not automatically film the complete training day. First establish what learners must understand or perform without a trainer, then choose the right medium for each element and plan publication, measurement and updates from the outset.
title: Turn training into video content: from course day to modular resource
metaDescription: Turn an existing training course into modular video content with a practical plan for learning goals, scripts, LMS delivery, accessibility, measurement and updates.
h1: Turn training into video content: from course day to modular knowledge product
factsToPreserve: A live training course is not automatically a ready-made video script
Not every element belongs in video; text, downloads, exercises, feedback and live sessions may serve the learning goal better
There is no universal ideal length for a training video
A representative pilot module should be tested before producing the whole series
Accurate captions need human review for specialist terms, names, numbers and negations
Accessibility covers the complete learner journey, not only the video file
Course completion means only what the LMS criteria have been configured to mean and does not prove durable skill
Viewing behaviour, completion and quiz results do not by themselves prove business, safety or performance outcomes
Copyright, image rights, privacy and confidential data require explicit review
Module codes, versions, owners, review dates and update triggers support maintainability
